summary: German-born Surrealist painter. Known for Two Children Are Threatened by a Nightingale, The Elephant Celebes, Ubu Imperator, and collage novels (A Week of Kindness, La Femme 100 Têtes). Invented frottage and grattage techniques. Alter ego named Loplop ('Superior of the Birds'). Husband of Dorothea Tanning; lover of Leonora Carrington.

works.name: Two Children Are Threatened by a Nightingale (1924)
works.description: Painting/collage hybrid. A man reaches for a red and blue knob on a roof while carrying a girl; an open gate and a knob protrude physically from the frame; a girl holds a knife up to a bird. Based on a childhood nightmare. Wikipedia

images.caption: Two Children Are Threatened by a Nightingale (1924)

clues.clue: An open gate and a knob protrude from the painting; a girl holds a knife up to a bird; man reaches for a knob on a roof

a man reaches towards a red and blue knob, and was based on a childhood nightmare

works.name: Collage Novels (A Week of Kindness, La Femme 100 Têtes)
works.description: Cut up Victorian pulp fiction illustrations to create collage novels. A Week of Kindness (Une Semaine de Bonté) is split into seven sections using Easter Island moai and lion heads.

comprehensive_summary:
Max Ernst (1891-1976) was a German-born Surrealist painter and sculptor. His most frequently clued work is Two Children Are Threatened by a Nightingale (1924), a painting/collage hybrid where an open gate and a knob protrude from the frame, a girl holds a knife at a bird, and a man reaches for a knob on a roof. He called it 'a kind of farewell' to his collage work.

He created collage novels by cutting up Victorian pulp fiction: A Week of Kindness (Une Semaine de Bonté) is split into seven sections using Easter Island moai and lion heads, and La Femme 100 Têtes features his alter ego Loplop, 'Superior of the Birds.' He invented frottage (rubbing pencil on uneven surfaces) and grattage (scratching paint off canvas). Joan Miró helped him develop grattage.

Other works include The Elephant Celebes, Ubu Imperator (with a main figure modeled on a Konkomba corn bin), Fireside Angel (monster forms a swastika), The Petrified City, and Murdering Airplane (collage with airplane with human arms). He married Dorothea Tanning and earlier had a ménage à trois with Gala and Paul Éluard. He collected Kachina dolls while living in Arizona.
